title: Formal vs Informal Register
description: Matching vocabulary, syntax, and tone to the situation: formal emails vs chats vs speeches.
summary_pt_br: Registro formal: verbos latinos, sem contrações, menos phrasal verbs. Informal: phrasal verbs, contrações, frases curtas.

scenes.subtitle_en: Matching vocabulary, syntax, and tone to the situation: formal emails vs chats vs speeches.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Registro formal: verbos latinos, sem contrações, menos phrasal verbs. Informal: phrasal verbs, contrações, frases curtas.

scenes.text_en: Matching vocabulary, syntax, and tone to the situation: formal emails vs chats vs speeches. Today's focus: formal vs informal register. We regret to inform you that your application was unsuccessful.
scenes.text_pt: Registro formal: verbos latinos, sem contrações, menos phrasal verbs. Informal: phrasal verbs, contrações, frases curtas. Foco de hoje: formal vs informal register. Lamentamos informar que sua candidatura não foi aprovada.

scenes.title: Formal vs Informal Register
scenes.body_en: Formal: Latinate verbs (investigate, purchase, assist), no contractions, fewer phrasal verbs. Informal: phrasal verbs (look into, buy, help), contractions, shorter sentences.
scenes.body_pt: Registro formal: verbos latinos, sem contrações, menos phrasal verbs. Informal: phrasal verbs, contrações, frases curtas.

examples.en: We regret to inform you that your application was unsuccessful.
examples.pt: Lamentamos informar que sua candidatura não foi aprovada.

examples.en: Sorry, we couldn't take you on this time.
examples.pt: Desculpa, não deu pra te contratar dessa vez.

examples.en: Please purchase the equipment by end of quarter.
examples.pt: Favor adquirir o equipamento até o fim do trimestre.

scenes.title: Key vocabulary — Formal vs Informal Register

exercise.question: Complete: "We regret to inform you that your application was ___."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "We regret to inform you that your application was unsuccessful."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Lamentamos informar que sua candidatura não foi aprovada.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Sorry, we couldn't take you on this time.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "Sorry, we couldn't take you on this time."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Desculpa, não deu pra te contratar dessa vez.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Formal vs Informal Register — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre formal vs informal register. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Rephrase this formally: 'We'll get back to you soon.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: We will respond to you shortly.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Informal version: 'We regret to inform you of the delay.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Formal version of: 'Can you look into this issue?'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Could you please investigate this matter?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Make this casual: 'Please purchase the materials.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Formal for: 'Thanks for helping out yesterday.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Register is just clothing for your sentences.

scenes.title: Two emails, one message
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Rewrite a short informal message into formal English. Use at least three register shifts.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Zara handed her students two emails that said the exact same thing in wildly different ways.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: One email said 'Hey, sorry we can't take you on this time.' The other was far more formal.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: And the formal one used 'We regret to inform you that your application was unsuccessful.'

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Both are correct — but each belongs to a different relationship between writer and reader.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: So register is about who I am writing to, not about which words are fancier?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Tone, Zara often said, is the invisible first draft every reader judges before the content.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Formal vs Informal Register". Matching vocabulary, syntax, and tone to the situation: formal emails vs chats vs speeches. We regret to inform you that your application was unsuccessful. Sorry, we couldn't take you on this time. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Formal vs Informal Register". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Formal vs Informal Register".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
